title: Write your own logit mask
tagline: In Pie the logit mask is user code. JSON Schema is a built-in, and the Constrain trait lets you write your own state machine when the built-ins do not fit.
question: Bio: "Hi, I am Bob, bob@x.io, 35yo." — extract {name, email, age}.

naive.label: Naive
naive.sublabel: free decode (no response_format) → strict JSON parse → retry on failure

events.value: without response_format, the model wraps the JSON in markdown fences
✗ failed after 3 attempts  •  66 tokens wasted  •  about 360 ms

pie.label: Pie
pie.sublabel: constrain_with(JsonSchema) — built in; or write your own Constrain impl

events.value: matcher constrains the JSON payload; user code extracts the first complete object and stops
valid payload on the first attempt — extracted before the fence closes
✓ 1 attempt  •  21 decoded (0 wasted)  •  about 158 ms  •  no retry

runCommand: pie run demo-grammar -- --bio "Hi, I'm Bob, bob@x.io, 35yo."
